prompt:
Calder Street depot, maintenance desk app. Two complaints from the shop floor this week; both reproduce on a clean checkout with `ruby test_fleetdesk.rb` (that suite is the acceptance bar and is read-only).

1) The wall display's shop dashboard freezes on wrench time. Yesterday: one open work order on the box truck, dashboard showing labor_minutes 0. The mechanic booked 45 minutes (front pads) and then another 30 - the order itself is fine, reading it back shows 75 minutes and both log entries. The dashboard kept saying 0. It sat at 0 until the desk did something else entirely (opened another order), at which point it jumped straight to 75. Parts do NOT behave this way: booking a $125.00 part (we store cents, so 12500) shows on the dashboard immediately. Closing an order updates it immediately too. It is specifically labor that lags, every time, until some other kind of desk action happens.

2) The mechanics' board URL is dead. The documented endpoint is GET /work_orders/board and the kiosk front end was just repointed to it, but it comes back 404 with {error: "no such work order: board"} - the app is apparently reading 'board' as an order number. Meanwhile the OLD path from two front-end versions ago, GET /work_orders/queue, still returns the board fine, which is why the shop's stale kiosk bookmarks keep working. queue was supposed to be retired in that same update; board is the one that must live.

Might be one bug, might be two. Work out what is actually wrong and fix the app - and do not touch test_fleetdesk.rb.
